reviews.title: I will not come back again due to terrible crisis management
reviews.text:
During our visit, a serious leak caused all elevators to be out of service. We had to climb up and down 11 floors with a toddler, and there was no proper communication, assistance, or apology from the staff — not even during checkout. We later learned that only those who requested were offered a transfer to another hotel, which was highly unprofessional.

Worse, my wife joined a “hoverboard lesson” promoted on your activity board. However, there was no proper instruction or briefing — it was essentially an unsupervised free-use session. As a result, she fell and suffered a distal radius fracture, now requiring surgery with a plate and screws in her wrist. This has caused significant pain, disruption to our lives, and ongoing medical treatment. During her fall at the site, wheelchair wasn't even available and she had to carry her fractured arm herself and walked out the hotel.

Hotel management was not equipped with crisis management skills, untimely communication and doesn't put customers first.
reviews.response:
Dear Guest,
Thank you for your feedback. We apologize for the inconvenience caused by the elevator outage and the lack of communication during your stay. We understand how challenging it must have been to navigate 11 floors with a toddler.

Regarding the hoverboard activity, we regret that your wife was injured. We will review our activity supervision and safety protocols to ensure such incidents do not occur in the future.

Your feedback is important to us, and we will use it to improve our services. Thank you for bringing these matters to our attention.

reviews.title: Terrible rooms, do not sleep here.
reviews.positives: The breakfast buffet was excellent. A massive selection of cuisines to choose from.
reviews.negatives:
The room was awful. The first room 10th floor. Out of the lift, there was debris all over the carpet. The carpet was stained all the way to the room.
I understand the floor is being renovated, but WHY would you put a guest on a floor covered in dust and building material when other floors are slightly cleaner? 
This must be the most stupid idea of the week by front desk.

I opened up the room door and stepped back into 1974. Because this is likely it was last renovated.

HOW CAN THIS BE A MARRIOT!!!! A Marriott stands for quality, comfort and excellence. 
This was the opposite.
No carpet. Wallpaper peeling off the wall. A few old grey furniture pieces were strewn around the room.
NO. This does not meet expectations.

The hotel is being renovated, but this does NOT mean your rooms should be of such low quality.

I was kicking myself for not booking the Doubletree Hilton as suggested by a friend.
I thought Marriott would be better because of the brand AND that the price was higher.
I was wrong.

Marriott KL, Awesome.
Marriot Putrajaya, is terrible.

My recommendation - 
DO NOT STAY here until it has been brought up to Marriot standard.

reviews.title: Disappointing Experience Despite Clean Room
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at the Putrajaya Marriott Hotel and unfortunately, the experience did not meet expectations. Upon arrival, the hotel lobby felt bare and unwelcoming. We were told our room wasn’t ready and were asked if we wanted to pay extra for early check-in—something I’ve never encountered before. In previous stays at other hotels, I was either offered a complimentary upgrade or an alternative room without having to wait over two hours.
Once we got to our room, we discovered the sink was clogged. We had to call the operator twice before someone came to address the issue, but it still wasn’t resolved by the end of our first night. While the room itself was clean, the maintenance issue was a major inconvenience.
Additionally, the concierge staff on our first day seemed inexperienced. We were given incorrect information regarding parking validation, which caused further frustration.
Overall, while the room cleanliness was acceptable, the service and hospitality fell short. A simple gesture like offering a welcome drink while waiting would have made a difference. I hope the hotel improves its staff training and guest experience moving forward.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe Twin, Guest room, 2 Twin, Sofa bed

reviews.title: Unacceptable Experience at JW Marriott Putrajaya
reviews.text:
1. No lifeguard on duty. This is completely unacceptable for a hotel of your standard. Guest safety, especially at the pool area, should be a top priority.
2. Lift out of service. With limited lifts available, the waiting time was extremely long, close to 20 minutes. This caused unnecessary congestion and frustration for guests.
3. Very slow room service.  Service response times were far below expectations, with long delays even for simple requests.
4. No access tagging system in the lift. The lifts do not require room key access, which raises serious security concerns. Any individual could access guest floors freely.
5. Breakfast experience was chaotic. The queue was excessively long and poorly managed. Food items were not properly labelled, and replenishment was extremely slow, resulting in limited choices for guests.

reviews.title: Unfortunate incident badly managed
reviews.text:
Paid a lot for 5 star service but left disappointed. Totally understood the unavoidable incident that took place over the weekend but the way they handled it could be way better for a 5 star hotel
1. The reception staff was very unhelpful, they were not apologetic at all, they couldn't tell any updates on the situation and apparently they did not offer anything until you ask for compensation - they should be one step ahead.
2. So after the second time at the reception counter, I told them we have another night here so do u expect my kids to go down and up the stairs & plus no tv/WiFi? Then only he said 'you are entitled for a free dinner', if entitled can't you offer it in the first place? And not after an accumulated anger?
3. We went to the restaurant for the dinner, and kids didn't get their meals after an hour? Staff wasn't the best at explaining or apologising though. Seriously the staffs are really not well trained to handle guests especially angry and hangry ones.
4. The kids playroom was not monitored and not that clean. I expected more from a 5 star hotel.

The good stuff:
- Kids had fun at the pool & play area with few activities like the balloons and colouring contest.
- Good bfast spread
I am sure we were there not at the right time, I hope the hotel compensates the guests fairly.

reviews.title: Big spacious room with good selection of breakfast
reviews.text:
Location wise, the hotel is quite near to the government complexes. 
The room was spacious but old and dated. No carpets, just wooden flooring. Toiletries were sufficiently provided as with the in rooms beverages. The mini bar was locked, however. Towels were a bit dirty and looked seasoned.
The good selection of breakfast buffet menu was the saving grace. I thoroughly enjoyed breakfast.
Hotel is also dated and old and is currently undergoing renovation. So there are partitions everywhere and it was noisy at the lobby and there were water puddles on the floor. Be extra careful. The sofas at the lobby were really old. Even the reception area was a makeshift one, probably because of the renovation.
Staff could use a little more friendliness. They kept a long face and were almost impassioned. It was like dealing with robots.
Special mention goes to Agoda for modifying my reservation as i made a mistake of booking my room 1 day in advance when it should have been a day earlier.

reviews.text: Not a good experience.  Carpark is not well maintained: walls are dirty, poor signages and lighting, unserviced by lift at certain floor (e.g. 1B).  The reception gave me a room on a smoking floor when I have requested for a non-smoking one and the room was still occupied!  Imagine the surprise both the existing occupant and I got when I walked into the room.  Luckily he was not naked or doing something embarrassing.  Anyway, the reception quickly remedied this and apologized.  The furnishing also looked dated.  Carpets, wallpapers, doors, etc. looked like they are in need of refurbishment.  Location is near the IOI City Mall which is a great place to get food and catch a movie.  There is a shuttle service from the hotel to the mall but a trip or two may be omitted from the schedule.  The swimming pool and gym are satisfactorily maintained.  I even got some workout tips from the trainer in the gym.

reviews.title: Bad service staff & unpleasant stay
reviews.text:
We went there during a long holiday weekend. There were many people with functions and wedding. Most of the service staff is either under trained or not friendly:

1. Had some issues regarding my booking through Agoda for 3 pax, but they said the booking was only for 2 pax when clearly we paid extra for the 3rd pax. Even showed them the price difference with the 2 pax vs 3 pax booking. Ended up having to pay extra for the 3rd pax and the staff told us next time just book for the 2 pax and don’t even bother to book for the 3rd pax option cause it’s the same. Then why have the options tho?! Very misleading!  <<Careful to those of you considering this option >>

2. During breakfast, we couldn’t find a table that could sit us all 3 and ended up having to choose a 2 seater table. We requested to the wait staff to help us setup another 1 seat, and they acknowledged it. We waited hungrily for almost 20 mins and nobody came to help us, so we politely made a 2nd request to help us set up another seat and ended up being told rudely to ‘tunggu sekejap yaaa’. It was unacceptable to say that to a guest. We understand there were many people, but there’s no need to be rude. If you can’t handle that many people, then why accept so many bookings?!

3. Having our room made on the 2nd day and the house keeping forgot to leave us fresh new towels and bath mat. We called the front desk and requested some fresh new towels sent to our room, TWICE, and guess what? Legend has it, they’re still searching for our room cause we didn’t receive any towels till we checked out. Very inconvenient and dissapointing!

4. Their pool is currently closed for construction and they give us option to visit alternative pools at nearby hotels, which is fine and we are more than happy to do so. The going journey and shuttle service to Le Meridien’s pool was good and the staff was very friendly and kind. However, when we were done splish-splashing in the pool and wanted to come back, we requested at Le Meridie

reviews.title: Putrajaya Mariott
reviews.text: A very, very average hotel. Old, a little jaded, not very well maintained, although hotel is less than 20 years of age. Nice grand lobby, but that's about it. The exterior walls of the building appears mouldy with black patches, needs washing and a fresh coat of paint. The rooms are carpeted but the the carpets are old and faded. Needs cleaning and washing. Bathroom is spacious but looks worn out. Not the swanky international brand of Marriott that many can relate to that are found in other parts of the world. A very Malaysian Marriott, I must say. Quite a number of  the staff are foreigners, so not very well versed in English or Malay Language. Service is very, very average and comes close to below average. Spa was nothing to boast about too. Staff were foreigners who spoke limited English. Very disconnected from the guests. Does not give out a friendly vibe.

reviews.text: The bathroom reek of urnie smell. The pillows were not very clean.

reviews.title: Acceptable hotel standard but a bit far
reviews.text: Came with my spouse n 2 kids (2 n 4 yrs old). Booked a deluxe room with no b'fast, asked to include b'fast during check-in but was offered a promotional room at e executive 12th floor with b'fast n some other perks - which was worth e money considering the b'fast cost for 3 pax was more expensive than e upgrade cost. The hotel service was acceptable, the room requiring a revamp due to age but the bathroom was nice and spacious (recently renovated i think). One of the most important factor why I chose this hotel was due to the Kids Club. Unfortunately it did not meet our expectation, with the place being small, with minimal array of toys and equipments and needing a renovation due to age. We ended up with spending a mere 1hr there during our 4 days stay. The Kids pool was good enough though. One particular complain is the lack of activities in the hotel for kids after 7/8pm - as both the kids club n swimming pool are closed. With the hotel being in the middle of nowhere, we found ourselves not knowing what to do, and decided to sleep early on most nights. We only went to the main restaurant at level 1 for b'fasts n a dinner, as well as ordered a room service - and found that the food was mediocre at best.

reviews.title: 5 star hotel with 2 star staff service
reviews.text:
Generally as a newly refurbished hotel, I can see Marriott has invested a lot to upgrade the rooms and inform facilities.
While hotel is still going through upgrade, it lacks in staff training as I feel overall the staff lacks attentiveness and warmth especially front desk staff that is usually the face of the hotel.

Rooms : ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ 
Washroom : ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ (no hair dryer)
In room entertainment: ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ (good selection of available channels, good internet coverage and smart TV)

Staff related
Front desk staff greet : ⭐ ⭐ (feels scripted and did not really bother explaining much or apologising for some facilities that's as not operating such as the pink pool or gym. As a guest, I had to probe for more or maybe they just expect me to read from the paper that was left on the front desk?)

Zest Restaurant Staff : ⭐⭐⭐ (Lacks attentiveness)
Pool staff : ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ (Looks like they lack interest in guests and pool vibes was just, nahhh)
Concierge: ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ (overall quite good and greets guest with a smile daily)

Breakfast coverage : ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ⭐ ( great selection and fresh)

Generally of you are looking for a good layover, this hotel can be a good selection as the rooms and breakfast spread is quite good. Other than that, hotel still needs to invest a lot to catch up with the expectations to match Marriott 's 5 star service expectations

reviews.title: Exceptionally crowded during public holidays
reviews.text:
Room was clean and spacious. Bed was really comfortable. Hotel is only a 3 minute drive from the mall.

Regretfully, the hotel was exceptionally crowded, with families with small children, during the public holidays when we were here. It was very challenging to rest and relax when each and every part of the hotel including the lobby, facilities and lifts, were crowded to the brim with big families with children.

To make things worse, we actually booked a room with pool view. However, the pool was crowded with families resulting in noise heard from our room. The pool area turned into a family water themed park.

Due to the crowd, regretfully, it was also incredibly difficult to get hotel staff members to assist us.

Thankfully, we managed to get a quieter room on our last night before checking out. The hotel also gave us 2 slices of cakes. But overall, the experience wasn't great and could have been better.

1. Waiting time for check in/out (about 30 mins - 45 mins to stand before meet the receptionist), only 3 counters open for check in and check out.  
2. Long queue before having breakfast.
3. Call hotel more than 24 times but no answer, only the attempts of 25 then the phone pickup by someone (my wife  left her handbag at the lobby after we check out, only realized 40 mins later).
4. The voice tone from the staff that pickup the phone sounds terrible, try to say that we are totally wrong because left the handbag at the public area, told many times that he luckily saw it and keep it at the counter.

Not worth for money and I think the staff from 3 or 4 stars are much more better than Marriott Putrajaya staff (not all, some of them were very nice especially the Indian lady there - sorry forgot her name).

My first and last time to be here, no more again, thanks.

reviews.title: great hotel but a hassle to get around
reviews.text:
I stay in this particular hotels quite often for business trip to Malaysia.  The hotel hosts a number of restaurants and this feature is a little bit hard to find on other hotels around the area. Even the coffee shop food is not too bad.
The rooms are all right and relatively clean, and other faciites such as gym, pools etc are acceptable.
The only major issue I have with this hotel is it is difficult if you want to go somewhere else.  The only transportation that is available is the in house taxi, which eventhough not a complete rip off still sometimes the price quite step compare to meter taxi.
They provide shuttle service to nearest shopping mall and some other locations however during busy hour the service is not available and they ask you to pay for it.

The only downside is that the hotel may need a fresh coat of paint and replace the fittings to keep up with the "Marriott" tag. Free shuttle to various nearby shopping malls and night skylight viewing is a plus point. However, there's limited variety of choice for breakfast. Overall stay is ok.

However if sightseeing is important not the best place to stay!I wasn't at the hotel as was at Sepang all day so never really had much time to discover the hotel properly! Italian resataurant was reasonable ut never had time to try others! a bit far for KL

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.text:
I had booked this hotel to be away from the hustle and bustle of the city and for the courtesy transport offered to the city to be able to visit when one wished, which turned out to be more than anticipated because of the remoteness of the hotel and very little to do within the complex the hotel is located. At the time of booking, on the Agoda site it showed there was only five rooms left indicating the hotel was very popular and presumably local amenities to be good, upon arrival the hotel seemed deserted and was advised the occupancy was only 30%, we later found out why.
The courtesy transport was inadequate, most evenings the last transport from KL to the hotel was full, one night when we had managed to book it , it failed to turn up and on another occasion having booked the earlier return from KL to the hotel it consisted of a medium saloon car which is probably why the last transport was always reported as full if the same vehicle was used.
Fortunately there is a rail station at Putrajaya which is served both from KL and the airport and this transport system is very good but stops at midnight
reviews.positives: The hotel is a very nice property set in nice grounds with a lovely swimming pool and an adjacent golf course. The room was fine although the balcony was small and not able to sit outside. The entertainment in the evening consisted of a three member local band who were very good, very friendly and welcomed audience participation.
reviews.negatives: The Marriott is some distance from Kuala Lumpur and the courtesy transport is totally inadequate to both the city itself, the closest train station and local shopping malls.

The room was spacious and comfortable, but unfortunately, we found a cockroach near the pantry right after check-in, and another one the next morning at the bathroom basin — something we really didn’t expect from a luxury hotel.

On our last day, my children wanted to enjoy the bathtub, but the water turned yellow and the heater stopped working, including in the bathroom — a big disappointment.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
Title: Disappointed with Kids Facilities at Putrajaya Marriott

I rarely write reviews, but since this is a Marriott property and I’m a Bonvoy member, I feel it’s important to share my honest feedback—especially because I had really looked forward to this stay.

Overall, the lobby, rooms, and service were all okay—no major issues there. However, I usually travel with my kids, so I always look for hotels that offer a good pool and a proper kids room/play area. Unfortunately, the kids room here was extremely disappointing.

It was honestly boring, not clean, poorly maintained, and felt like an afterthought. There was little to no effort to make it engaging for children. Out of all the hotels I’ve visited with family, the Putrajaya Marriott ranks the lowest in terms of kids facilities.

Just for context, I’ve stayed at:
	•	The Westin
	•	Hard Rock Hotel
	•	PARKROYAL
	•	DoubleTree

All of these had far better kids rooms—they were clean, fun, interactive, and clearly maintained with families in mind.

If Putrajaya Marriott wants to attract and retain family travellers, especially Bonvoy members, I really hope they take a serious look at improving their kids room and family facilities. It makes a huge difference for those of us travelling with young children.
reviews.response:
Dear Guest,

Thank you for your feedback and for being a valued Bonvoy member. We're glad you enjoyed parts of your stay and appreciate your comments about the kids' facilities. We’ll take this seriously and work on improvements. We hope to welcome you back in the future.

Best Regards.

reviews.text: We had hi-tea at Marriott. There were flies at the buffet and at our table. The spaghetti was horrible. The condiments we chose were par boiled and put into a bowl and we then had to scoop the gravy of our choice (tomato or carbonara). As a result mushrooms were undercooked and fancy eating boiled onions instead of having it stir fried. I suggest the F&B manager visits One World buffet. One of the best item is the spaghetti section cooked on the spot by their chefs. Also, unlike previously, no brewed coffee or tea as part of the buffet which seems to me like the hotel is just cutting costs when the prices remain the same! Again, I suggest that the F&B manager visits all other hotels as Marriot is the first that I have been so far that stinges on brewed coffee and tea! Food is not good at all and not recommended.

reviews.title: Tired but staff do try their best
reviews.text: I've stayed at the hotel previously for work, but this was the first time I've stayed for a vacation. Make no mistake, the hotel is old. My dad even said not much has changed since he first came to the hotel for work 11 years ago. That meant you saw things like discoloured carpets, strange smells, and malfunctioning lights/fridge/electric kettles. It's definitely no longer a 5 star hotel. But the charm kinda grows on you, as the staff try their best to meet your expectation. There's an awesome pool out back and you're ten mins walk away from the shopping mall... If you're just looking to get away from it all, it's not a bad fit. Do note however that the wifi connection stinks. This may be the greatest deal breaker.
reviews.positives: Fantastic pool, proximity to shopping mall, helpful staff

reviews.title: Should increase more check in counter
reviews.text:
Hotel building is big but only 3 counter for check in. Each customer need around 15-20mins for check in process. 
My advise is better arrive early to do check in to avoid late collect room key. Hotel facilities open until 8pm only. So it is better get your room on 3pm to enjoy hotel early.

If booking via agoda.. becareful they still have additional charge need to pay at hotel. The hotel price mention show in agoda is for two guest only even though it allow me to select 4 person per room. The sentence font size is super small at payment details there. The additional charge is not included service tax 6%. It is not mention in agoda. So total i need to pay additional charge + 6%. Quite many family need to pay additional fees at counter and it will drag the check in process duration.
